title: Add pit-cycle cooldown and out-lap phase features
summary: After a car has just stopped, an immediate next-lap stop is usually structurally unlikely except for penalties/damage/weather.
rationale: After a car has just stopped, an immediate next-lap stop is usually structurally unlikely except for penalties/damage/weather. Existing top solutions count prior stops but do not fully encode lap_since_last_pit, post-stop out-lap warm-up, or cooldown regimes.
implementation_hint: For each Race-Year/Driver sequence, derive laps_since_last_pit, laps_until_current_stint_start, previous_lap_pitstop, current_is_outlap_proxy, first_2_laps_after_stop, and long_stint_without_stop. Keep these causal and compute identically for train/test.
expected_effect: Should reduce false positives immediately after stops and sharpen hazards as a stint matures.
risk: If PitStop marks pit entry differently than expected, phase offsets may be off by one; test variants using current, lag-1, and lag-2 PitStop flags.
